Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.1 Purchase Orders; Domain 16.2 PO Intake
Discrepancies / Open Questions: Current documentation still contains older page naming and route assumptions that can hide the actual slice-plus-work-surface composition
User Job: Review the PO queue and the currently selected order with accurate receiving context
Capability: Browse purchase orders, inspect detail context, and switch between standard and receiving queue modes
Trigger: Open Purchase Orders, search, filter, or deep-link to a specific PO
Success Criteria: The correct PO list loads, receiving mode shows pending items, and selected-order context stays aligned to the active row
Permissions / Access Rules: purchase-order access; receiving access for receiving mode
Validations / Guards: Search, status filter, and deep-link selection must remain trustworthy across standard and receiving modes
Outputs / Side Effects: Operators can still see the right POs and receiving context before taking action
Hidden Dependencies: Mode switching; supplier name resolution; deep-link state
Failure Mode if Lost: Purchasing loses the queue truth needed to place, inspect, or receive orders safely
Evidence Required: Queue proof; filter proof; selected-context proof

Discrepancies / Open Questions: The recording barely reviewed PO visuals, so current draft behavior must take precedence over mock assumptions
User Job: Create a purchase order draft with the right supplier, dates, payment terms, notes, and initial lines
Capability: Preserve PO creation and draft editing as a real working document flow
Trigger: Open the create dialog or edit a draft PO
Success Criteria: Draft rows, dates, payment terms, notes, and supplier context remain consistent from creation through later edits
Current UI Surface: PurchaseOrdersWorkSurface create dialog and draft surface
Validations / Guards: A supplier is required; at least one valid line item is required; deleted drafts cannot linger invisibly
Outputs / Side Effects: PO drafts persist and remain editable until intentionally removed or advanced
Hidden Dependencies: Supplier resolution; payment terms; notes continuity; draft-state transitions
Failure Mode if Lost: Purchasing workflow breaks before inventory even reaches receiving
Parity Test Requirement: PO create, edit, and delete regression
Evidence Required: Draft proof; supplier proof; delete proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: purchaseOrders.create; purchaseOrders.update; none found in matrix for inline draft-row selection, duplication, deletion, or keyboard focus movement
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The spreadsheet-native value of the current PO surface depends on keyboard-friendly drafting that the artboards did not spell out
User Job: Work through many draft lines quickly without losing row-level control
Capability: Preserve inline line-item drafting, duplication, deletion, validation, and keyboard movement
Trigger: Add a line, duplicate selected rows, remove selected rows, or move focus across draft cells
Success Criteria: Line-item work remains fast, explicit, and validated without collapsing into a modal-only flow
Validations / Guards: Keep at least one draft row; line validation errors stay tied to the correct row; keyboard navigation cannot skip validation silently
API / Service Dependencies: current PO draft state; row validation helpers
Outputs / Side Effects: Draft lines remain editable, duplicable, removable, and keyboard-friendly
Hidden Dependencies: Typed-product resolution; row focus management; validation state
Failure Mode if Lost: PO drafting becomes slower and more error-prone even if the backend API still exists
Evidence Required: Line-edit proof; duplicate proof; delete-row proof; keyboard proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: none found in matrix for bulk quantity mode, bulk COGS mode, or export helper behavior
Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.1 Purchase Orders; Domain 19.1 Cost of Goods Sold
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The pack under-signaled bulk editing, notes, and export even though they are real operator capabilities today
User Job: Update many lines efficiently while preserving cost context and internal notes
Capability: Preserve bulk quantity, bulk COGS, internal notes, totals, and CSV export support
Trigger: Select multiple draft rows, apply bulk changes, edit notes, or export a PO
Success Criteria: Bulk updates stay explicit, totals remain coherent, and exported line items still reflect the selected PO
Current UI Surface: PurchaseOrdersWorkSurface bulk controls, notes, totals, and export action
Validations / Guards: Bulk changes must only target selected draft rows; fixed and range COGS must validate correctly; export must use the intended PO line set
Outputs / Side Effects: Bulk maintenance, notes, totals, and exports remain available
Hidden Dependencies: COGS-mode rules; internal notes; export formatting
Failure Mode if Lost: PO upkeep becomes slow, opaque, or hard to hand off downstream
Parity Test Requirement: Bulk-edit, notes, totals, and export regression
Evidence Required: Bulk proof; notes proof; totals proof; export proof

Discrepancies / Open Questions: The real status lifecycle is richer than a single Place Order button and must stay explicit
User Job: Advance a PO through submission and downstream status changes without breaking trust
Capability: Preserve submission, confirmation, and status-transition behavior for purchase orders
Trigger: Place an order, confirm it, or change status intentionally
Success Criteria: Only valid state transitions succeed, and the PO status remains accurate for later receiving work
Permissions / Access Rules: purchase-order access; PO write; manager-only access for some status changes
Current UI Surface: PurchaseOrdersSlicePage placement actions; PurchaseOrdersWorkSurface status actions
Validations / Guards: Submit only from DRAFT; confirm only from SENT; status updates remain explicit and reversible where current UI supports them
Outputs / Side Effects: POs advance through the right state changes and remain ready for receiving
Hidden Dependencies: Status lifecycle semantics; supplier confirmation notes; downstream receiving dependence
Failure Mode if Lost: POs appear placed or confirmed when the real purchasing lifecycle has broken
Evidence Required: Place-order proof; confirm proof; status-change proof

Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.1 Purchase Orders; Domain 16.2 PO Intake
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The visual pack can make PO sheets feel self-contained, but real receiving adjacency is part of the current workflow
User Job: Move a submitted PO cleanly into receiving with the right quantities and provenance
Capability: Preserve pending-receiving visibility and the handoff from PO into intake or receiving work
Trigger: Open a receive-eligible PO and start receiving
Success Criteria: Only receive-eligible lines hand off, the created intake draft uses the correct PO context, and PO state stays synchronized with receiving
Current UI Surface: PurchaseOrdersSlicePage receiving mode; PurchaseOrdersWorkSurface Open Receiving Queue action
Validations / Guards: Receiving quantities cannot exceed the remaining quantity; branch routing must preserve PO provenance; receiving must stay visibly adjacent instead of disappearing
Data Entities: purchase_orders; purchase_order_lines; intake drafts; inventory batches
Outputs / Side Effects: Submitted PO lines remain actionable in receiving and create the correct next-state draft
Hidden Dependencies: Cross-workbook handoff; quantity remaining logic; receiving route family
Failure Mode if Lost: Purchase Orders stops connecting to real intake and inventory creation loses provenance
Sidecar / Exception Need: Receiving remains an explicit adjacent step
Evidence Required: Pending-receiving proof; intake-draft proof; receiving proof
